quality.rationale: 相对论粘性流体应力张量+垂直投影守恒律+多阶小量近似+成功还原经典流体力学方程

origin.question: From the stress-energy$$\mathbf {T}^{\alpha \beta} = \rho \mathbf {u}^{\alpha} \mathbf {u}^{\beta} + \mathrm {p P}^{\alpha \beta} - 2 \eta \sigma^{\alpha \beta} - \zeta \theta \mathbf {P}^{\alpha \beta},$$show that the equations of motion derived from $\mathbf{T}^{\alpha \beta},_\beta = 0$ reduce to the Navier-Stokes equations in the nonrelativistic limit.
origin.solution: Solution 5.31. We know that projecting $\mathbf{T}^{\alpha \beta}_{,\beta}$ along $\mathfrak{u}_\alpha$ gives an equation of local energy conservation; to get an equation of motion for the fluid, we project perpendicular to $\mathfrak{u}_\alpha$ .$$\begin{array}{l} 0 = \mathrm {P}_{\alpha}^{\gamma} \mathrm {T}^{\alpha \beta} ,_\beta = \mathrm {P}_{\alpha}^{\gamma} ( \rho ,_\beta \mathrm {u}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}^{\beta} + \rho \mathrm {u}^{\alpha} ,_\beta \mathrm {u}^{\beta} + \rho \mathrm {u}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}^{\beta} ,_\beta + \mathrm {p} ,_\beta \mathrm {P}^{\alpha \beta} \\+ \mathrm {p P}^{\alpha \beta} ,_\beta - 2 \eta ,_\beta^{\sigma^{\alpha \beta}} - 2 \eta^{\sigma^{\alpha \beta}} ,_\beta - \zeta ,_\beta^{\theta \mathrm {P}^{\alpha \beta}} - \zeta \theta ,_\beta^{\mathrm {P}^{\alpha \beta}} - \zeta \theta \mathrm {P}^{\alpha \beta} ,_\beta ) . \\ \end{array}$$We now use the following identities: $\mathbf {P}_{\alpha}^{\gamma}\mathfrak{u}^{\alpha} = 0$$$\begin{array}{l} \mathrm {P}_{\alpha}^{\gamma} \mathrm {u}_{,\beta}^{\alpha} = \mathrm {u}_{,\beta}^{\gamma} + \mathrm {u}_{\alpha}^{\gamma} \mathrm {u}_{,\beta}^{\alpha} = \mathrm {u}_{,\beta}^{\gamma} \\ \mathbf {P}_{a}^{\gamma} \mathbf {P}^{a \beta} = \mathbf {P}^{\gamma \beta} \\ \end{array}$$$$\begin{array}{l} \mathrm {P}_{\alpha}^{\gamma} \mathrm {P}_{,\beta}^{\alpha \beta} = \mathrm {P}_{\alpha}^{\gamma} ( \mathrm {u}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}^{\beta} ) ,_\beta = \mathrm {u}^{\gamma}_{,\beta} \mathrm {u}^{\beta} \\ \mathrm {P}_{\alpha}^{\gamma} \sigma^{\alpha \beta} = \sigma^{\gamma \beta} \\ \end{array}$$$$\mathbf {P}_{\alpha}^{\gamma} \sigma^{\alpha \beta}_{,\beta} = \sigma^{\gamma \beta}_{,\beta} + \mathfrak {u}^{\gamma} \mathfrak {u}_{\alpha} \sigma^{\alpha \beta}_{,\beta} = \sigma^{\gamma \beta}_{,\beta} - \mathfrak {u}^{\gamma} \mathfrak {u}_{\alpha}_{,\beta} \sigma^{\alpha \beta} = \sigma^{\gamma \beta}_{,\beta} - \mathfrak {u}^{\gamma} \sigma_{\alpha} \beta \sigma^{\alpha \beta} .$$With these identities the projected equations of motion become:$$\begin{array}{l} 0 = ( \rho + p ) u^{\gamma}_{,\beta} u^{\beta} + p_{,\beta} P^{\alpha \beta} - 2 ( \eta \sigma^{\gamma \beta} + \zeta \theta P^{\gamma \beta} )_{,\beta}+ 2 \eta \mathrm {u}^{\gamma} \sigma_{\alpha \beta} \sigma^{\alpha \beta} + \zeta \theta^{2} \mathrm {u}^{\gamma} . \tag {1} \\ \end{array}$$In the nonrelativistic limit,$$u^{t} \approx 1, \quad u^{j} \approx v^{j} , \quad p = \mathcal {O} (v^{2} ) , \quad \rho = \mathcal {O} ( 1 ) .$$Taking the j-component of Equation (1), and working to $\mathcal{O}(\mathbf{v}^2)$ , we get$$\begin{array}{l} 0 = \rho ( \mathrm {v}_{, t}^{\mathrm {j}} + \mathrm {v}_{, k}^{\mathrm {j}} \mathrm {v}^{\mathrm {k}} ) + \mathrm {p}_{, j}- \left[ \eta \left( \mathrm {v}_{\mathrm {j} , \mathrm {k}} + \mathrm {v}_{\mathrm {k} , \mathrm {j}} - \frac {2} {3} \delta_{\mathrm {j k}} \mathrm {v}^{\mathrm {m}} , \mathrm {m}\right)\right]_{, \mathrm {k}} + \left( \zeta \mathrm {v}^{\mathrm {m}} , \mathrm {m}\right)_{, \mathrm {j}} \\ \end{array}$$which is the Navier-Stokes equation.
